title: Sholeh Zard (Saffron Rice Pudding)
description: A fragrant, golden-yellow Persian rice pudding infused with saffron, rosewater, and cardamom, beautifully garnished with cinnamon and nuts.

ingredients.note: Ground, bloomed in 3 tbsp hot water

instructions.text: Drain the soaked rice. In a large heavy-bottomed pot, combine the rice and 6 cups of water. Bring to a boil, skimming off any foam that rises to the top.

Reduce heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for about 45-60 minutes until the rice is completely soft and broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. The mixture should look like a thick porridge.
Add the sugar to the pot and stir continuously until fully dissolved. The mixture will thin out once the sugar is added. Simmer for another 15 minutes.
Add the bloomed saffron and watch the pudding turn a vibrant golden yellow. Stir well.

Stir in the cracked cardamom pods, rosewater, butter, and the rosewater-soaked slivered almonds. Simmer gently for another 10-15 minutes until the pudding thickens to your liking. It should coat the back of a spoon.
Remove the cardamom pods. Pour the hot pudding into a large shallow serving dish or individual dessert bowls.

Let the pudding cool slightly at room temperature until a skin forms on top. Then, use ground cinnamon, slivered pistachios, and remaining almonds to create decorative patterns or calligraphy on the surface.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours to set completely before serving.

tips: For the best texture, use broken basmati rice or a starchy short-grain rice. If using long-grain basmati, you may need to pulse it a few times in a blender before cooking so it breaks down properly.
storage_notes: Store in the refrigerator covered with plastic wrap for up to 5 days. It is usually served cold, straight from the fridge.

substitutions.note: Makes the dessert fully vegan without significantly altering the flavor.
Use 1/2 tsp ground cardamom if you don't want to fish out whole pods.
